adversaries.name: SCN-001 | Credential Dumping via LSASS
adversaries.description: Simulates a threat actor dumping credentials from LSASS memory using native Windows tools (comsvcs.dll MiniDump). No external binaries required for the dump stage — classic living-off-the-land technique. Mapped to T1003.001. Commonly observed in MDR cases as a precursor to lateral movement.

adversaries.name: SCN-002 | Discovery & Enumeration Chain
adversaries.description: Simulates post-compromise discovery activity — the first thing an attacker does after gaining a foothold. Runs four sequential LOLBin techniques: system info collection (T1082), network configuration mapping (T1016), running process enumeration with security tool detection (T1057), and local user/gr

adversaries.name: SCN-003 | Phishing → Payload → Persistence
adversaries.description: Simulates a phishing-initiated compromise. Phase 1 (GoPhish email delivery) is narrated in the SE runbook — demo starts post-click with the CALDERA agent already running. Phase 2 establishes dual persistence: a scheduled task disguised as MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineCore (T1053.005) and a registry

adversaries.name: SCN-005 | Pass the Hash Lateral Movement
adversaries.description: Simulates post-compromise lateral movement using NTLM hashes extracted in a prior credential dumping stage. Enumerates network shares to identify targets, narrates SAM hash extraction, then demonstrates Pass the Hash authentication without a plaintext password. Represents the natural continuation of

adversaries.name: SCN-006 | PowerShell Execution & AMSI Bypass
adversaries.description: Simulates attacker abuse of PowerShell for execution and defense evasion. Step 1 demonstrates Base64-encoded command execution to bypass string-matching defenses. Step 2 narrates AMSI bypass via AmsiScanBuffer memory patching — explaining how attackers disable PowerShell's security scanning layer. S

adversaries.name: SCN-008 | Ransomware Pre-Deployment Simulation
adversaries.description: Simulates the four-phase pre-deployment sequence used by ransomware operators before encrypting files: disable backup services (VSS, WBEngine), narrate shadow copy deletion, stage a realistic ransom note and encrypted file markers, then summarize all detections for the Taegis handoff. No actual file

abilities.name: SCN-002 | Network Configuration Discovery
abilities.description:
Enumerates network interfaces, IP addresses, active connections, routing
table, and DNS configuration. Attacker uses this to map the internal network,
identify reachable hosts, and plan lateral moveme

abilities.name: SCN-002 | Running Process Discovery
abilities.description:
Enumerates running processes, identifies security tools (AV/EDR), and
looks for juicy targets (browsers with creds, password managers, VPN clients).
Attacker uses this to identify defenses and priorit

abilities.name: SCN-002 | System Information Discovery
abilities.description:
Collects OS version, hostname, domain membership, hardware specs, and
current user context. First thing an attacker does after gaining a foothold
— builds the target profile used to plan next steps.

abilities.name: SCN-002 | Local User and Group Enumeration
abilities.description:
Enumerates local user accounts, local groups, and Administrators group
members. Attacker uses this to identify privileged accounts for targeted
lateral movement or credential theft.

abilities.name: SCN-005 | Enumerate Network Shares
abilities.description:
Discovers accessible network shares on the local host and connected network
using native Windows commands. Attacker uses share enumeration to identify
file servers, admin shares (C$, ADMIN$), and data

abilities.name: SCN-003 | Phishing Payload Execution
abilities.description:
Simulates a user executing a phishing-delivered payload — the moment after
a malicious Office macro runs or a link is clicked. Narrates that GoPhish
handled the email delivery; this step represents th

abilities.name: SCN-006 | PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
abilities.description:
Executes a Base64-encoded PowerShell command to simulate the obfuscation
technique attackers use to bypass basic string-matching defenses. Encodes

abilities.name: SCN-006 | Simulated Payload Download and Execute
abilities.description:

abilities.name: SCN-003 | Establish Persistence via Registry Run Key
abilities.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
abilities.description:
Writes a Run key entry to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run
disguised as "OneDriveSync" to execute the implant at every user login.

abilities.name: SCN-003 | Establish Persistence via Scheduled Task
abilities.description:
Creates a scheduled task that re-runs the implant on user logon and
at system startup, ensuring the attacker maintains access even after
a reboot or session timeout. Uses native schtasks.exe — no exte

abilities.description:

abilities.name: SCN-008 | Disable Backup and Recovery Services
abilities.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
abilities.description:
Disables Volume Shadow Copy (VSS) and Windows Backup (WBEngine) services
to prevent recovery after ransomware encryption — a standard ransomware

abilities.name: SCN-001 | Check Current User Privileges
abilities.description:

abilities.name: SCN-001 | Dump LSASS via comsvcs.dll (MiniDump)
abilities.description:
Dumps LSASS process memory to disk using the built-in comsvcs.dll MiniDump export.
This is a living-off-the-land technique — no external tooling required.

abilities.name: SCN-001 | Extract Credentials via Mimikatz sekurlsa

NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets from LSASS memory. This is the high-
